items.title: These Were the 5 Worst-Performing Stocks in the Dow Jones Industrial Average in 2024
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 14% in 2024, but five stocks - Johnson & Johnson, Merck, Amgen, Nike, and Boeing - were the worst performers, all losing value over the year. The reasons for their underperformance vary, from pandemic-related issues to market share declines and CEO changes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Johnson & Johnson is a healthcare stock that often moves based on news about drug performance and new products. It pays dividends and is a dividend king, making it a reliable passive income option.
Merck is a healthcare stock that often moves based on news about drug performance and new products. It pays dividends, making it a reliable passive income option.
Amgen is a healthcare stock that often moves based on news about drug performance and new products. It pays dividends, making it a reliable passive income option.
Nike has let competitors cut into its market share and has seen its sales decline. It also just got a new CEO, and the market is waiting for another update.

Boeing has had many problems, starting with the more general pandemic-related issues like low demand and inflation, and there were major safety concerns last year. It culminated in a new CEO, and investors are waiting to see what's in store this year.
